titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Tereze Glück Papers
titlestmt.author: by Annalise Hennessey

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 00:59
Finding aid updated by Elise Riley to add separated material.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of materials by or relating to American author and businesswoman Tereze Glück, including correspondence, writings, and personal papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of materials by or relating to American author and businesswoman Tereze Glück, including correspondence, writings, and personal papers. Among the correspondents are her immediate family, friends, and colleagues, including her sister Louise Glück and journalist Michael Curtis. Writings consist of notes and drafts of Glück's short stories, poetry, articles, and other works, including materials relating to May You Live in Interesting Times , as well as notebooks. Personal papers contain materials such as Glück's diary and high school yearbook, photographs, newspaper clippings, and ephemera.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

bioghist.head: Tereze Glück (1945-2018)
bioghist.p: Tereze Glück was born on October 20, 1945 in Woodmere, Long Island, New York. She graduated from Vassar College and worked as Vice President of Citibank for over thirty years. Her collection of short stories May You Live in Interesting Times won the Iowa Short Fiction Award in 1994. She was also a frequent contributor to the New York Times Opinion section and various magazines and journals, including North American Review , Antioch Review , Gettysburg Review , Fiction , Epoch , and Story .

Glück was the younger sister of Louise Glück, a former U.S. Poet Laureate, Yale Rosenkranz Writer-in-Residence, and winner of the 2020 Nobel Prize in Literature. Glück's daughter is actor Abigail Savage. Gluck died on December 16, 2018 in New York City

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Tereze Glück Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Tereze Glück Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1955-2014. II. Writings, 1963-2018, undated. III. Personal Papers, 1946-2018, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence between Tereze Glück and friends, family, and colleagues, including Michael Curtis, Jeffrey Bader, Larry Kramer, and Louise Glück.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or file title, with unidentified correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes drafts, notebooks, essays, and other writings by Tereze Glück.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Shorter Works, Poetry, and Notebooks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes essays, short stories, and other shorter works written by Glück.

did.unittitle: "Doublets in the Gospel of Thomas Compared with their Synoptic Parallels"
"Essence and Quintessence or, I Can Make You Love Yourself"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes drafts and notes concerning Glück's published and unpublished poetry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes journals, notebooks, and planners concerning Glück's works and personal life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes Glück's original watercolor drawings, diary, photographs, high school yearbook, and other personal papers.
